pokemon:goodra.name: Goodra
pokemon:goodra.summary: It attacks with retractable horns. It throws a punch that’s the equivalent of the force of a hundred pro boxers.
expansionNotes: Competitive, encounter, and media coverage can be expanded later without changing the page contract.

pokedexEntries.text: It attacks with retractable horns. It throws a punch that’s the equivalent of the force of a hundred pro boxers.
Able to freely control the hardness of its metallic shell. It loathes solitude and is extremely clingy—it will fume and run riot if those dearest to it ever leave its side.
It gets picked on because it’s meek. But then, whoever teased it gets to feel the full force of its horns and a good swatting from its thick tail.
This very friendly Dragon-type Pokémon will hug its beloved Trainer, leaving that Trainer covered in sticky slime.
Its form of offense is forcefully stretching out its horns. The strikes land 100 times harder than any blow from a heavyweight boxer.
An amazingly friendly Pokémon, but if left to itself, loneliness overcomes it, and it oozes gooey tears.
Sometimes it misunderstands instructions and appears dazed or bewildered. Many Trainers don’t mind, finding this behavior to be adorable.
Underneath its slimy skin is a thick layer of fat, and the combination of the two makes enemies’ attacks slide right off.
It’s very friendly toward people. If you grow close to it, Goodra will hug you with its sticky, slime-covered body. Don’t get mad.
This very friendly Dragon-type Pokémon will hug its beloved Trainer, leaving that Trainer covered in sticky slime.
It attacks with retractable horns. It throws a punch that’s the equivalent of the force of a hundred pro boxers.

competitiveSummary: Competitive notes are pending a richer source import.
loreSummary: It attacks with retractable horns. It throws a punch that’s the equivalent of the force of a hundred pro boxers.

pokemon:sliggoo.name: Sliggoo
pokemon:sliggoo.summary: Its four horns are a high-performance radar system. It uses them to sense sounds and smells, rather than using ears or a nose.
expansionNotes: Competitive, encounter, and media coverage can be expanded later without changing the page contract.

pokedexEntries.text: Its four horns are a high-performance radar system. It uses them to sense sounds and smells, rather than using ears or a nose.
A creature given to melancholy. I suspect its metallic shell developed as a result of the mucus on its skin reacting with the iron in Hisui's water.
This Pokémon’s mucous can dissolve anything. Toothless, it sprays mucous on its prey. Once they’re nicely dissolved, it slurps them up.
It drives away opponents by excreting a sticky liquid that can dissolve anything. Its eyes devolved, so it can’t see anything.
The lump on its back contains its tiny brain. It thinks only of food and escaping its enemies.
It has trouble drawing a line between friends and food. It will calmly try to melt and eat even those it gets along well with.
Although this Pokémon isn’t very strong, its body is coated in a caustic slime that can melt through anything, so predators steer clear of it.
Its entire body is covered with gooey mucus that can dissolve anything. Be sure to put gloves on if you’re going to touch it.
It crawls along sluggishly. The swirly protrusion on its back is filled with its brain and other organs.
It drives away opponents by excreting a sticky liquid that can dissolve anything. Its eyes devolved, so it can’t see anything.
Its four horns are a high-performance radar system. It uses them to sense sounds and smells, rather than using ears or a nose.

competitiveSummary: Competitive notes are pending a richer source import.
loreSummary: Its four horns are a high-performance radar system. It uses them to sense sounds and smells, rather than using ears or a nose.

pokemon:goomy.name: Goomy
pokemon:goomy.summary: It’s covered in a slimy membrane that makes any punches or kicks slide off it harmlessly.
expansionNotes: Competitive, encounter, and media coverage can be expanded later without changing the page contract.

pokedexEntries.text: It’s covered in a slimy membrane that makes any punches or kicks slide off it harmlessly.
Goomy hides away in the shade of trees, where it's nice and humid. If the slime coating its body dries out, the Pokémon instantly becomes lethargic.
Its source of protection is its slimy, germ-laden mucous membrane. Anyone who touches it needs some thorough hand-washing.
The weakest Dragon-type Pokémon, it lives in damp, shady places, so its body doesn’t dry out.
Their horns are powerful sensors. As soon as Goomy pick up any sign of enemies, they go into hiding. This is how they’ve survived.
The weakest of all Dragon-type Pokémon, it’s unable to breathe if its skin dries out, so it sticks to shady places.
Because most of its body is water, it will dry up if the weather becomes too arid. It’s considered the weakest dragon Pokémon.
Its body is mostly water. If it dries out, it will die, so it’s always sitting with perfect stillness in the shade.
It uses its horns to check out its surroundings. They’re very sensitive, so if you grab them, it will feel a strong shock and be unable to move.
The weakest Dragon-type Pokémon, it lives in damp, shady places, so its body doesn’t dry out.
It’s covered in a slimy membrane that makes any punches or kicks slide off it harmlessly.

competitiveSummary: Competitive notes are pending a richer source import.
loreSummary: It’s covered in a slimy membrane that makes any punches or kicks slide off it harmlessly.
